title: How to make a date column not be a due date
excerpt:
I am new to Monday but cant find the answer to this anywhere. 
I have a date column I am using just to show the date entered. My problem is when I look at my work it is placing all of those as past due when I simply want…

posts.author: clgrimes (Cheryl)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am new to Monday but cant find the answer to this anywhere.

I have a date column I am using just to show the date entered. My problem is when I look at my work it is placing all of those as past due when I simply want it to indicate the date I added the item

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: In “My Work” click Customize at the top. In the right-hand pane that opens you will see “Date Column” and within that, a list of the boards and which date column to use for the due date. You can choose “no selection” and it won’t have any due date (and will stay at the bottom of my work with other undated items) or you can pick the correct date column to use as a due date.

posts.author: clgrimes (Cheryl)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank You so much!!! I was driving myself crazy figuring that out. You just made my day
